name: Multi-stage onboarding (Folder+Loop+AI+DB+Excel+Alert)
description: Plan-to-commands evaluation captured from production log on 2026-04-30 at 16:03:37.
user_prompt:
Convert this workflow plan to JSON commands:

Workflow Plan: Customer Onboarding

Important implementation note: there is no trigger/watch node in this platform. To satisfy the user's request to "watch C:\\incoming\\onboarding", build the workflow below starting with a Folder Selector node, and after the workflow is built, create a separate schedule or external trigger to run it on the desired cadence.

1. Add a Folder Selector node named "Select Onboarding PDFs".
   - Purpose: Select PDF files from C:\\incoming\\onboarding.
   - Configuration:
     - folderPath: C:\\incoming\\onboarding
     - selectionMode: pattern match
     - filePattern: *.pdf
     - failIfNoFilesFound: false
     - Output variable: onboardingFiles

2. Add a Set Variable node named "Initialize Collected Rows" immediately after the Folder Selector.
   - Purpose: Create an empty array to accumulate extracted customer rows for Excel Export.
   - Configuration:
     - variableName: collectedRows
     - mode: expression
     - value/expression: []

3. Add a Loop node named "Loop Through PDF Files".
   - Purpose: Iterate through each selected PDF file.
   - Configuration:
     - itemsVariable: ${onboardingFiles}
     - itemVariableName: currentFile
     - indexVariableName: currentIndex
     - maxIterations: 10000

4. Inside the loop, add an AI Extract node named "Extract Customer Fields".
   - Purpose: Extract structured customer data directly from each PDF file.
   - Configuration:
     - input: ${currentFile}
     - output target: variable
     - outputVariable: extractedCustomer
     - Fields (preserve exactly):
       - customer_name | type: text | description: Customer full name found in the onboarding PDF.
       - customer_email | type: text | description: Customer email address found in the onboarding PDF.
     - Use reasonable defaults for extraction confidence and model behavior.

5. Inside the loop, add a Database node named "Insert Customer Into Database" using connection ID 5.
   - Purpose: Insert the extracted data into an existing table on connection ID 5 using the closest matching existing columns discovered from schema.
   - Schema-based table choice:
     - Connection ID 5 is valid.
     - No table on connection ID 5 contains both exact columns customer_name and customer_email.
     - Use dbo.AgentEmailAddresses because it contains email_address and from_name, which are the closest existing columns for customer_email and customer_name.
   - Configuration:
     - connection: "5"
     - saveToVariable: true
     - outputVariable: dbInsertResult
     - SQL query (preserve exactly):
       INSERT INTO dbo.AgentEmailAddresses (email_address, from_name, is_active, created_at)
       VALUES (${extractedCustomer.customer_email}, ${extractedCustomer.customer_name}, 1, GETDATE());

6. Inside the loop, add a Set Variable node named "Build Current Row".
   - Purpose: Create a row object for downstream Excel export using extracted values plus source file context.
   - Configuration:
     - variableName: currentRow
     - mode: expression
     - value/expression: {"customer_name": extractedCustomer.get("customer_name"), "customer_email": extractedCustomer.get("customer_email"), "source_file": currentFile}

7. Inside the loop, add a Set Variable node named "Append Row To Collection".
   - Purpose: Append the current row to the collectedRows array.
   - Configuration:
     - variableName: collectedRows
     - mode: expression
     - value/expression: collectedRows + [currentRow]

8. Add an End Loop node named "End File Loop".
   - Purpose: Close the loop.
   - Configuration:
     - Exactly one outgoing pass connection back to the Loop node "Loop Through PDF Files".

9. After the loop completes, add an Excel Export node named "Write Onboarded Excel File".
   - Purpose: Export all collected customer rows to an Excel file.
   - Configuration:
     - excelOperation: create
     - excelOutputPath: onboarded.xlsx
     - source data variable: ${collectedRows}
     - Use auto-mapping with these expected columns:
       - customer_name
       - customer_email
       - source_file
     - outputVariable: excelExportResult

10. Add a Set Variable node named "Prepare Email Body" after Excel Export.
    - Purpose: Create a simple variable for the Alert node message body because Alert only accepts a simple variable reference.
    - Configuration:
      - variableName: alertMessage
      - mode: expression
      - value/expression: f"Customer onboarding workflow completed. Processed {len(collectedRows)} PDF file(s). Excel output: onboarded.xlsx"

11. Add a Set Variable node named "Prepare Email Subject".
    - Purpose: Create a simple variable for the Alert node email subject.
    - Configuration:
      - variableName: alertSubject
      - mode: expression
      - value/expression: "Customer onboarding completed"

12. Add an Alert node named "Email CRM Team".
    - Purpose: Send completion notification to crm@acme.com.
    - Configuration:
      - alertType: Email
      - recipients: crm@acme.com
      - emailSubject: ${alertSubject}
      - messageTemplate: ${alertMessage}
      - attachmentFilePath: onboarded.xlsx
      - Use reasonable defaults for sender/profile.

13. Connections:
    - Select Onboarding PDFs -> Initialize Collected Rows
    - Initialize Collected Rows -> Loop Through PDF Files
    - Loop Through PDF Files (loop body/pass) -> Extract Customer Fields
    - Extract Customer Fields -> Insert Customer Into Database
    - Insert Customer Into Database -> Build Current Row
    - Build Current Row -> Append Row To Collection
    - Append Row To Collection -> End File Loop
    - End File Loop (single pass connection) -> Loop Through PDF Files
    - Loop Through PDF Files (done/exit) -> Write Onboarded Excel File
    - Write Onboarded Excel File -> Prepare Email Body
    - Prepare Email Body -> Prepare Email Subject
    - Prepare Email Subject -> Email CRM Team

14. Post-build note:
    - To make this truly "watch" C:\\incoming\\onboarding, create a separate platform schedule or external trigger to run this workflow automatically at the desired interval.
